definition: A long piece of writing on a subject, especially for a university degree.
definitionHe: חיבור ארוך על נושא, במיוחד לצורך תואר אוניברסיטאי.
examples: She spent three years writing her dissertation.
His dissertation examined the effects of climate change.
The dissertation was published as a book.

commonMistakes.wrong: She wrote a dissertation about history
commonMistakes.correct: She wrote a dissertation on history
commonMistakes.explanation: אומרים dissertation on (לא about) כשמדברים על הנושא.

tip: Use 'dissertation on' (not 'about') when referring to the topic. In UK, 'thesis' is more common for PhD work.
tipHe: אומרים dissertation on (לא about) כשמתייחסים לנושא. בבריטניה, thesis נפוץ יותר לדוקטורט.

definition: The subjects and content taught in a school or educational program.
definitionHe: הנושאים והתוכן הנלמדים בבית ספר או תוכנית חינוכית.
examples: The school updated its curriculum to include coding.
The national curriculum sets educational standards.
She designs curriculum for language programs.

commonMistakes.explanation: הרבים של curriculum הוא curricula (לטינית), לא curriculums.

tip: Plural: 'curricula' (Latin) or 'curriculums' (both accepted, but 'curricula' is more formal). 'CV' = curriculum vitae.
tipHe: רבים: curricula (לטינית) או curriculums (שניהם מקובלים, אבל curricula רשמי יותר). CV = curriculum vitae.

definition: The method and practice of teaching.
definitionHe: שיטת ההוראה ותרגולה.
examples: Modern pedagogy emphasizes critical thinking.
She studied pedagogy at the university.
Digital pedagogy uses technology in teaching.

commonMistakes.wrong: The pedagogy of the teacher is good
commonMistakes.explanation: עם pedagogy אומרים effective/innovative (לא good). גם עדיף שייכות: teacher's pedagogy.

tip: Use 'effective/innovative pedagogy' (not 'good'). The adjective is 'pedagogical'. A teacher is a 'pedagogue'.
tipHe: אומרים effective/innovative pedagogy (לא good). התואר: pedagogical. המורה: pedagogue.

definition: A system of methods used in a particular field of study.
definitionHe: מערכת של שיטות המשמשות בתחום מחקר מסוים.
examples: The research methodology was clearly described.
They adopted a new methodology for data collection.
Methodology is a key section in academic papers.

commonMistakes.wrong: The methodology of the study were explained
commonMistakes.correct: The methodology of the study was explained
commonMistakes.explanation: methodology הוא יחיד → was.

tip: 'Methodology' is singular → 'was'. Don't confuse 'method' (a single technique) with 'methodology' (a system of methods).
tipHe: methodology הוא יחיד → was. אל תבלבלו method (טכניקה בודדת) עם methodology (מערכת שיטות).

definition: Based on observation and experience rather than theory.
definitionHe: מבוסס על תצפית וניסיון ולא על תיאוריה.
examples: Empirical evidence supports the hypothesis.
The study uses an empirical approach.
Empirical data is essential for scientific research.

commonMistakes.explanation: evidence היא לא-ספירה - אין צורת רבים. evidence shows (לא evidences show).

tip: 'Evidence' is uncountable in English - never 'evidences'. Say 'pieces of evidence' for plural meaning.
tipHe: evidence היא לא-ספירה באנגלית - אף פעם לא evidences. אומרים pieces of evidence לרבים.

definition: To suggest or assume something as a basis for reasoning.
definitionHe: להציע או להניח משהו כבסיס לטיעון.
examples: Einstein postulated the theory of relativity.
The philosopher postulated the existence of free will.
Scientists postulate that the universe is expanding.

commonMistakes.explanation: postulate הוא יוצא - postulate something (ללא about).

tip: 'Postulate' is transitive: 'postulate something' (no 'about'). As a noun, stress shifts: POST-yoo-lut.
tipHe: postulate הוא יוצא: postulate something (ללא about). כשם עצם, ההדגשה עוברת: POST-yoo-lut.

definition: Involving serious academic study; relating to scholars.
definitionHe: כרוך בלימוד אקדמי רציני; הקשור למלומדים.
examples: She published several scholarly articles.
The book is based on scholarly research.
Scholarly journals require peer review.

commonMistakes.explanation: שים לב לאיות: scholarly (עם ly בסוף).

tip: 'Scholarly' = academic/serious. Don't confuse with 'scholastic' (relating to schools). A 'scholar' is a learned person.
tipHe: scholarly = אקדמי/רציני. אל תבלבלו עם scholastic (הקשור לבתי ספר). scholar הוא אדם מלומד.

definition: Involving two or more academic disciplines or fields of study.
definitionHe: כולל שני תחומי לימוד אקדמיים או יותר.
examples: The program offers interdisciplinary courses.
Interdisciplinary research combines science and art.
An interdisciplinary approach yields richer results.

commonMistakes.explanation: interdisciplinary נכתבת כמילה אחת, ללא מקף.

tip: 'Interdisciplinary' is one word (no hyphen). Related: 'multidisciplinary' (many fields) vs 'interdisciplinary' (between fields).
tipHe: interdisciplinary היא מילה אחת (ללא מקף). קשור: multidisciplinary (תחומים רבים) מול interdisciplinary (בין תחומים).

definition: Official recognition that an institution meets required standards.
definitionHe: הכרה רשמית שמוסד עומד בסטנדרטים הנדרשים.
examples: The university received accreditation from the board.
Accreditation ensures quality in education.
The program lost its accreditation after the review.

commonMistakes.explanation: אחרי doesn't הפועל בצורת בסיס: have (לא has).

tip: After 'doesn't', use the base form: 'doesn't have' (not 'doesn't has'). Basic but frequently violated rule.
tipHe: אחרי doesn't, משתמשים בצורת בסיס: doesn't have (לא doesn't has). כלל בסיסי שמופר לעתים קרובות.

definition: An outline of the topics covered in a course.
definitionHe: תוכן הנושאים הנלמדים בקורס.
examples: The syllabus lists all assignments and deadlines.
The professor distributed the syllabus on the first day.
The syllabus was updated to include new topics.

commonMistakes.explanation: הרבים של syllabus הוא syllabi (לטינית) או syllabuses.

tip: Plural: 'syllabi' (formal/Latin) or 'syllabuses' (informal). Both are accepted. Related to 'curriculum' but more specific.
tipHe: רבים: syllabi (רשמי/לטינית) או syllabuses (לא רשמי). שניהם מקובלים. קשור ל-curriculum אבל ספציפי יותר.

definition: A statement or theory put forward to be supported; a long academic work.
definitionHe: הצהרה או תיאוריה שמוצגת לגיבוי; עבודה אקדמית ארוכה.
examples: She defended her thesis before the committee.
The thesis argues that language shapes thought.
His thesis was on artificial intelligence.

commonMistakes.wrong: The thesis of the student were accepted
commonMistakes.explanation: thesis הוא יחיד → was. הרבים: theses.

tip: Singular: 'thesis'. Plural: 'theses' (Greek). In essays, 'thesis statement' = your main argument.
tipHe: יחיד: thesis. רבים: theses (יוונית). בחיבורים, thesis statement = הטענה המרכזית שלך.

definition: The practice of taking someone else's work and presenting it as your own.
definitionHe: הנוהג של לקיחת עבודה של מישהו אחר והצגתה כשלך.
examples: Plagiarism can result in expulsion from university.
Software can detect plagiarism in written work.
She was accused of plagiarism in her paper.

commonMistakes.explanation: אומרים commit plagiarism (לא do/make). plagiarism היא גם לא-ספירה (ללא a).

tip: 'Commit plagiarism' (not 'do'). It's uncountable - no 'a'. The verb is 'plagiarize'. The person: 'plagiarist'.
tipHe: אומרים commit plagiarism (לא do). לא-ספירה - ללא a. הפועל: plagiarize. האדם: plagiarist.

definition: Something required before something else can happen.
definitionHe: משהו שנדרש לפני שמשהו אחר יכול לקרות.
examples: Math 101 is a prerequisite for this course.
Experience is a prerequisite for the position.
Meeting the prerequisites is mandatory for enrollment.

commonMistakes.wrong: This is a prerequisite to the course
commonMistakes.correct: This is a prerequisite for the course
commonMistakes.explanation: אומרים prerequisite for (לא to).

tip: Use 'prerequisite for' (not 'to'). 'Pre-' means 'before', 'requisite' means 'required'. Together: required beforehand.
tipHe: אומרים prerequisite for (לא to). pre- = לפני, requisite = נדרש. ביחד: נדרש מראש.

definition: A story with a hidden meaning, usually moral or political.
definitionHe: סיפור עם משמעות נסתרת, בדרך כלל מוסרית או פוליטית.
examples: Animal Farm is an allegory about totalitarianism.
The allegory uses animals to represent political figures.
Medieval literature is rich in allegory.

commonMistakes.wrong: The book is an allegory about the politics
commonMistakes.correct: The book is an allegory for politics
commonMistakes.explanation: אומרים allegory for/of (לא about).

tip: Use 'allegory for/of' (not 'about'). The adjective is 'allegorical'. Allegory differs from metaphor in being a complete narrative.
tipHe: אומרים allegory for/of (לא about). התואר: allegorical. אלגוריה שונה ממטאפורה בכך שהיא סיפור שלם.

definition: The main character in a story, play, or film.
definitionHe: הדמות הראשית בסיפור, מחזה או סרט.
examples: The protagonist faces many challenges.
She is the protagonist of the novel.
The protagonist's journey drives the plot.

commonMistakes.wrong: The protagonists of the movie is a hero
commonMistakes.correct: The protagonist of the movie is a hero
commonMistakes.explanation: protagonist כבר אומר דמות ראשית. main protagonist הוא pleonasm (כפילות).

tip: 'Main protagonist' is redundant - 'protagonist' already means the main character. The opposite is 'antagonist'.
tipHe: main protagonist הוא כפילות - protagonist כבר אומר דמות ראשית. ההפוך: antagonist.

definition: A figure of speech comparing two unlike things without using 'like' or 'as'.
definitionHe: תמונת דיבור המשווה שני דברים לא דומים ללא שימוש ב-like או as.
examples: Life is a journey is a common metaphor.
The poet uses powerful metaphors.
She extended the metaphor throughout the essay.

commonMistakes.explanation: 1) metaphor ללא e בסוף. 2) אומרים use a metaphor (לא make).

tip: Spelling: 'metaphor' (no 'e'). Use 'use/employ a metaphor' (not 'make'). Compare with 'simile' (uses 'like/as').
tipHe: איות: metaphor (ללא e). אומרים use/employ a metaphor (לא make). השוו עם simile (משתמש ב-like/as).

definition: A spoken or written account of connected events; a story.
definitionHe: תיאור מדובר או כתוב של אירועים קשורים; סיפור.
examples: The film presents a compelling narrative.
She challenged the dominant narrative.
First-person narrative creates intimacy with the reader.

commonMistakes.wrong: He told a narrative about his life
commonMistakes.correct: He told a narrative of his life
commonMistakes.explanation: אומרים narrative of (לא about) כשמתייחסים לתוכן.

tip: 'Narrative of' is more literary than 'narrative about'. Also used as adjective: 'narrative technique'. Narrator = the one who tells.
tipHe: narrative of ספרותי יותר מ-narrative about. משמש גם כתואר: narrative technique. narrator = המספר.

definition: Written or spoken language in ordinary form, without metrical structure.
definitionHe: שפה כתובה או מדוברת בצורה רגילה, ללא מבנה מטרי.
examples: His prose style is elegant and clear.
The novel is written in beautiful prose.
Prose differs from poetry in its structure.

commonMistakes.explanation: prose היא לא-ספירה - אין צורת רבים. אומרים good prose.

tip: 'Prose' is uncountable - never 'proses'. It means any non-poetry writing. 'Prosaic' (adj) = ordinary/dull.
tipHe: prose היא לא-ספירה - אף פעם לא proses. פירושה כל כתיבה שאינה שירה. prosaic (תואר) = רגיל/משעמם.

definition: Writing arranged with a metrical rhythm; a stanza of a poem.
definitionHe: כתיבה מסודרת בקצב מטרי; בית של שיר.
examples: She recited a verse from the poem.
The song has three verses and a chorus.
He published a collection of verse.

commonMistakes.explanation: verses הוא רבים - a לא מתאים לרבים. אומרים beautiful verses (ללא a).

tip: Without 'a': 'beautiful verses' (plural). With 'a': 'a beautiful verse' (singular). 'Verse' can also mean a section of the Bible.
tipHe: ללא a: beautiful verses (רבים). עם a: a beautiful verse (יחיד). verse יכול גם לאמר פסוק בתנ"ך.

definition: The use of humor or irony to criticize people or society.
definitionHe: שימוש בהומור או אירוניה לביקורת על אנשים או חברה.
examples: The show is a brilliant satire of politics.
Swift's works are famous for their satire.
Social satire can influence public opinion.

commonMistakes.wrong: He wrote a satir about the government
commonMistakes.correct: He wrote a satire about the government
commonMistakes.explanation: שים לב לאיות: satire עם e בסוף.

tip: 'Satire' = the genre/work. 'Satirical' = adjective. 'Satirize' = verb. 'Satirist' = the person who writes it.
tipHe: satire = הז'אנר/היצירה. satirical = תואר. satirize = פועל. satirist = הכותב.

definition: A category of artistic work characterized by a particular style.
definitionHe: קטגוריה של יצירה אמנותית המאופיינת בסגנון מסוים.
examples: Science fiction is my favorite genre.
The film blends multiple genres.
Each literary genre has its own conventions.

commonMistakes.wrong: Which genre of movie do you like?
commonMistakes.correct: Which genre of film do you like?
commonMistakes.explanation: genre of film (לא movie) בהקשר ספרותי/רשמי. בדיבור רגיל, שניהם מקובלים.

tip: 'Genre' is from French - the pronunciation is ZHON-ruh (not JEN-ree). Plural: 'genres'. Works for any art form.
tipHe: genre מצרפתית - ההגייה ZHON-ruh (לא JEN-ree). רבים: genres. מתאים לכל צורת אמנות.

definition: An imagined society in which there is great suffering and injustice.
definitionHe: חברה מדומיינת שבה יש סבל ואי-צדק רבים.
examples: 1984 portrays a terrifying dystopia.
Dystopian fiction has become very popular.
The movie depicts a dystopia ruled by machines.

commonMistakes.explanation: שים לב לאיות: dystopia (לא dystopy). מיוונית: dys (רע) + topos (מקום).

tip: 'Dystopia' = opposite of 'utopia'. The adjective is 'dystopian'. From Greek: dys (bad) + topos (place).
tipHe: dystopia = ההפך מ-utopia. התואר: dystopian. מיוונית: dys (רע) + topos (מקום).

definition: A speech in a play where a character speaks their thoughts aloud, alone.
definitionHe: נאום במחזה שבו דמות אומרת את מחשבותיה בקול רם, לבד.
examples: Hamlet's 'To be or not to be' is a famous soliloquy.
The actress delivered the soliloquy with great emotion.
Shakespeare's plays contain many soliloquies.

commonMistakes.explanation: שים לב לאיות: soliloquy (לא soliloguy). מלטינית: solus (לבד) + loqui (לדבר).

tip: Tricky spelling: 'soliloquy'. From Latin 'solus' (alone) + 'loqui' (to speak). Different from 'monologue' - a soliloquy is to oneself.
tipHe: איות מורכב: soliloquy. מלטינית solus (לבד) + loqui (לדבר). שונה מ-monologue - soliloquy הוא לעצמו.

definition: The placement of two things side by side for comparison or contrast.
definitionHe: הצבת שני דברים זה לצד זה לצורך השוואה או ניגוד.
examples: The juxtaposition of wealth and poverty is striking.
The artist uses juxtaposition to create contrast.
The juxtaposition highlights the differences between the characters.

commonMistakes.wrong: The juxtaposition among the two ideas
commonMistakes.correct: The juxtaposition of the two ideas
commonMistakes.explanation: אומרים juxtaposition of (לא among/between).

tip: Use 'juxtaposition of A and B' (not 'between' or 'among'). The verb is 'juxtapose'.
tipHe: אומרים juxtaposition of A and B (לא between או among). הפועל: juxtapose.

definition: A literary device giving hints about future events in a story.
definitionHe: כלי ספרותי שנותן רמזים על אירועים עתידיים בסיפור.
examples: The dark clouds in chapter one are foreshadowing.
The author uses foreshadowing to build suspense.
Early foreshadowing made the ending less surprising.

commonMistakes.explanation: כשם עצם, צריך foreshadowing (לא foreshadow). foreshadow הוא הפועל.

tip: 'Foreshadow' = verb. 'Foreshadowing' = noun/gerund. The prefix 'fore-' means 'before' (foreword, forecast, foresee).
tipHe: foreshadow = פועל. foreshadowing = שם עצם. הקידומת fore- = לפני (foreword, forecast, foresee).

definition: The use of symbols to represent ideas or qualities.
definitionHe: שימוש בסמלים לייצוג רעיונות או תכונות.
examples: The white dove is a symbolism of peace.
Symbolism is a key element in poetry.
The novel is rich in religious symbolism.

commonMistakes.wrong: The white color is a symbolism of peace
commonMistakes.correct: The white color is a symbol of peace
commonMistakes.explanation: symbol = סמל (דבר שמייצג). symbolism = סמליות (השימוש בסמלים). צריך symbol כאן.

tip: 'Symbol' = the specific thing representing something. 'Symbolism' = the practice/technique of using symbols.
tipHe: symbol = הדבר הספציפי שמייצג. symbolism = הנוהג/הטכניקה של שימוש בסמלים.

definition: Fluent, forceful, and persuasive speaking or writing.
definitionHe: דיבור או כתיבה שוטפים, חזקים ומשכנעים.
examples: Her eloquence captivated the entire audience.
The lawyer's eloquence swayed the jury.
He was known for his eloquence in debate.

commonMistakes.explanation: eloquence היא לא-ספירה - ללא a.

tip: 'Eloquence' is uncountable - no 'a'. The adjective is 'eloquent': 'an eloquent speaker'.
tipHe: eloquence היא לא-ספירה - ללא a. התואר: eloquent: an eloquent speaker.

definition: The act of convincing someone to do or believe something.
definitionHe: הפעולה של שכנוע מישהו לעשות או להאמין במשהו.
examples: She used gentle persuasion to change his mind.
The art of persuasion is essential in marketing.
He resisted all attempts at persuasion.

commonMistakes.explanation: המשפט 'persuaded with persuasion' הוא חזרה. אומרים convinced through persuasion.

tip: Avoid tautology: don't say 'persuaded with persuasion'. Use different verbs: 'convinced through persuasion'.
tipHe: הימנעו מטאוטולוגיה: אל תאמרו persuaded with persuasion. השתמשו בפעלים שונים: convinced through persuasion.

definition: A mistaken belief or a flaw in reasoning.
definitionHe: אמונה שגויה או פגם בחשיבה.
examples: The argument contains a logical fallacy.
It's a fallacy that money buys happiness.
She identified the fallacy in his reasoning.

commonMistakes.wrong: It's a fallacy to think like that
commonMistakes.correct: It's a fallacy to think that way
commonMistakes.explanation: אומרים that way (לא like that) בסוף משפט באנגלית רשמית.

tip: 'It's a fallacy that...' or 'It's a fallacy to think...' are both correct. Common fallacies include ad hominem, straw man, and slippery slope.
tipHe: It's a fallacy that... או It's a fallacy to think... שניהם נכונים. כשלים נפוצים: ad hominem, straw man, slippery slope.

definition: Written or spoken communication or discussion.
definitionHe: תקשורת או דיון כתובים או מדוברים.
examples: Academic discourse follows specific conventions.
The political discourse has become toxic.
She contributes to the public discourse on education.

commonMistakes.explanation: אומרים discourse on (לא about) בהקשר אקדמי.

tip: In formal/academic writing, use 'discourse on' (not 'about'). As a verb: 'discourse on a subject' (formal).
tipHe: בכתיבה רשמית/אקדמית, אומרים discourse on (לא about). כפועל: discourse on a subject (רשמי).

definition: To express thoughts or feelings clearly and effectively.
definitionHe: להביע מחשבות או רגשות בצורה ברורה ויעילה.
examples: She articulated her position with clarity.
He struggled to articulate his feelings.
The report articulates the key findings.

commonMistakes.correct: She is a very articulate person
commonMistakes.explanation: חסר a לפני very articulate person.

tip: Pronunciation shifts: verb = ar-TIK-yoo-LAYT, adjective = ar-TIK-yoo-LUT. Both are common uses.
tipHe: ההגייה משתנה: פועל = ar-TIK-yoo-LAYT, תואר = ar-TIK-yoo-LUT. שני השימושים נפוצים.

definition: To prove that a statement or theory is wrong.
definitionHe: להוכיח שהצהרה או תיאוריה שגויים.
examples: The scientist refuted the claims with data.
His argument was difficult to refute.
She refuted the accusations in her speech.

commonMistakes.explanation: refute הוא יוצא - refute something (ללא against).

tip: 'Refute' = prove wrong (stronger). 'Deny' = say it's not true (weaker). Don't confuse them.
tipHe: refute = להוכיח שזה שגוי (חזק יותר). deny = לומר שזה לא נכון (חלש יותר). אל תבלבלו.

definition: To publicly support or recommend a cause or policy.
definitionHe: לתמוך באופן פומבי או להמליץ על מטרה או מדיניות.
examples: She advocates for equal rights.
Many doctors advocate a plant-based diet.
He advocated strongly for reform.

commonMistakes.explanation: אומרים advocate for (לא about).

tip: Verb: 'advocate FOR something' (ad-vuh-KAYT). Noun: 'an advocate' (AD-vuh-kut). Note the pronunciation difference!
tipHe: פועל: advocate FOR something (ad-vuh-KAYT). שם עצם: an advocate (AD-vuh-kut). שימו לב להבדל בהגייה!

definition: To provide evidence to support a claim or statement.
definitionHe: לספק ראיות לתמיכה בטענה או הצהרה.
examples: Can you substantiate your claims with evidence?
The report substantiates the theory.
She failed to substantiate her accusations.

commonMistakes.explanation: אחרי didn't הפועל בצורת בסיס: substantiate (לא substantiated).

tip: After 'didn't', always use base form: 'didn't substantiate' (not 'substantiated'). The adjective: 'unsubstantiated'.
tipHe: אחרי didn't, תמיד צורת בסיס: didn't substantiate (לא substantiated). התואר: unsubstantiated.

definition: An argument or statement that contradicts or opposes something.
definitionHe: טיעון או הצהרה שסותרים או מתנגדים למשהו.
examples: His rebuttal was sharp and well-prepared.
She offered a strong rebuttal to the criticism.
The lawyer's rebuttal convinced the jury.

commonMistakes.wrong: He gave a rebuttle to the argument
commonMistakes.correct: He gave a rebuttal to the argument
commonMistakes.explanation: שים לב לאיות: rebuttal עם al (לא le).

tip: Spelling: 'rebuttal' ends in '-al' (not '-le'). The verb is 'rebut' (rebutted, rebutting - double 't').
tipHe: איות: rebuttal מסתיים ב-al (לא le). הפועל: rebut (rebutted, rebutting - t כפולה).

definition: A short amusing or interesting story about a real event or person.
definitionHe: סיפור קצר משעשע או מעניין על אירוע או אדם אמיתיים.
examples: He told an anecdote about his childhood.
The speech was filled with amusing anecdotes.
An anecdote is not the same as data.

commonMistakes.explanation: שים לב לאיות: anecdote עם e בסוף.

tip: 'Anecdote' ends with '-e'. Don't confuse with 'antidote' (cure for poison). Adjective: 'anecdotal' (anecdotal evidence).
tipHe: anecdote מסתיימת ב-e. אל תבלבלו עם antidote (תרופה לרעל). תואר: anecdotal (ראיות אנקדוטליות).

definition: Exaggerated statements not meant to be taken literally.
definitionHe: הצהרות מוגזמות שלא נועדו להילקח כפשוטן.
examples: 'I'm so hungry I could eat a horse' is a hyperbole.
Politicians often use hyperbole in speeches.
The advertisement uses hyperbole to attract attention.

commonMistakes.wrong: The hyperbole in his speech was obvious
commonMistakes.explanation: hyperbole מבוטאת hy-PUR-buh-lee (4 הברות), לא HY-per-bohl.

tip: Pronunciation: hy-PUR-buh-lee (4 syllables, not 3). Don't say HY-per-bohl. The adjective is 'hyperbolic'.
tipHe: הגייה: hy-PUR-buh-lee (4 הברות, לא 3). אל תאמרו HY-per-bohl. התואר: hyperbolic.

definition: Open to discussion or argument; not certain.
definitionHe: פתוח לדיון או ויכוח; לא ודאי.
examples: Whether the policy works is debatable.
The results are highly debatable.
That claim is debatable at best.

commonMistakes.explanation: debated = שנדון (כבר). debatable = שניתן לדון בו (אפשרי). צריך debatable.

tip: 'Debatable' = open to debate. 'Debated' = already discussed. 'It's a debatable point' (can be argued).
tipHe: debatable = פתוח לדיון. debated = כבר נדון. It's a debatable point (ניתן לוויכוח).

definition: A diplomat who represents their country in another country.
definitionHe: דיפלומט שמייצג את מדינתו במדינה אחרת.
examples: The ambassador presented her credentials to the president.
He was appointed ambassador to France.
The ambassador called for peaceful negotiations.

commonMistakes.wrong: He was appointed as an ambassador to France
commonMistakes.correct: He was appointed ambassador to France
commonMistakes.explanation: בהקשר דיפלומטי רשמי, אומרים appointed ambassador (ללא as an).

tip: In formal diplomatic language: 'appointed ambassador to France' (no 'as an'). Also used figuratively: 'brand ambassador'.
tipHe: בשפה דיפלומטית רשמית: appointed ambassador to France (ללא as an). גם משמש באופן מטאפורי: brand ambassador.

definition: A formal agreement between countries.
definitionHe: הסכם רשמי בין מדינות.
examples: The two nations signed a peace treaty.
The treaty was ratified by both governments.
The treaty bans nuclear weapons testing.

commonMistakes.explanation: treaty (אמנה) ולא treat (פינוק/ממתק). מילים שונות!

tip: Don't confuse 'treaty' (agreement) with 'treat' (something enjoyable). Plural: 'treaties'.
tipHe: אל תבלבלו treaty (אמנה) עם treat (פינוק). רבים: treaties.

definition: Involving two parties, especially two countries.
definitionHe: כולל שני צדדים, במיוחד שתי מדינות.
examples: They signed a bilateral trade agreement.
Bilateral talks began last week.
The bilateral relationship has strengthened.

commonMistakes.wrong: The two countries have a bilateral relationships
commonMistakes.correct: The two countries have a bilateral relationship
commonMistakes.explanation: relationship ביחיד כשמדברים על היחסים בין שתי מדינות.

tip: 'Bilateral relationship' (singular) for one pair. 'Bi-' = two. Compare: 'multilateral' (many parties), 'unilateral' (one party).
tipHe: bilateral relationship (יחיד) לזוג אחד. bi- = שניים. השוו: multilateral (צדדים רבים), unilateral (צד אחד).

definition: The process of resolving a dispute with the help of a neutral third party.
definitionHe: תהליך של פתרון סכסוך בעזרת צד שלישי ניטרלי.
examples: The conflict was resolved through mediation.
They agreed to mediation instead of going to court.
International mediation helped end the crisis.

commonMistakes.explanation: אומרים agree to mediation (לא agree for).

tip: 'Agree to mediation' (not 'for'). The mediator is the neutral person. Compare: 'arbitration' (binding) vs 'mediation' (advisory).
tipHe: agree to mediation (לא for). המתווך הוא האדם הניטרלי. השוו: arbitration (מחייב) מול mediation (ייעוצי).

definition: An agreement to stop fighting temporarily.
definitionHe: הסכם להפסקת לחימה באופן זמני.
examples: Both sides agreed to a ceasefire.
The ceasefire was broken after two days.
They called for an immediate ceasefire.

commonMistakes.wrong: They agreed on a cease fire
commonMistakes.explanation: ceasefire נכתבת כמילה אחת. גם אומרים agreed to (לא on).

tip: 'Ceasefire' is one word. Use 'agree to a ceasefire' or 'call for a ceasefire'. Compare: 'truce' (similar meaning).
tipHe: ceasefire היא מילה אחת. אומרים agree to a ceasefire או call for a ceasefire. השוו: truce (משמעות דומה).

definition: To formally approve or confirm an agreement, especially by a government.
definitionHe: לאשר באופן רשמי הסכם, במיוחד על ידי ממשלה.
examples: Parliament ratified the treaty unanimously.
The agreement must be ratified by all member states.
The constitution was ratified in 1788.

commonMistakes.explanation: כשמוסיפים ed לפועל שמסתיים ב-y, מחליפים y ב-ied: ratified.

tip: When adding '-ed' to verbs ending in consonant + y, change y to i: ratify → ratified. The noun: 'ratification'.
tipHe: כשמוסיפים ed לפעלים שמסתיימים בעיצור + y, מחליפים y ב-i: ratify → ratified. שם העצם: ratification.

definition: A person sent by a government on a diplomatic mission.
definitionHe: אדם שנשלח על ידי ממשלה במשימה דיפלומטית.
examples: The UN sent a special envoy to the region.
The envoy delivered the message to the foreign minister.
A peace envoy was dispatched to negotiate.

commonMistakes.wrong: The envoy went for a diplomatic trip
commonMistakes.correct: The envoy went on a diplomatic mission
commonMistakes.explanation: אומרים on a mission (לא for a trip) בהקשר דיפלומטי.

tip: An 'envoy' is lower-ranking than an 'ambassador'. Use 'on a mission' (not 'for a trip') in diplomatic context.
tipHe: envoy הוא בדרגה נמוכה יותר מ-ambassador. אומרים on a mission (לא for a trip) בהקשר דיפלומטי.

definition: Relating to diplomacy; tactful and skilled in dealing with people.
definitionHe: הקשור לדיפלומטיה; טקטי ומיומן בעבודה עם אנשים.
examples: She gave a diplomatic response to the criticism.
Diplomatic relations between the countries improved.
He was very diplomatic in handling the situation.

commonMistakes.wrong: He was very diplomatic in his answer
commonMistakes.explanation: diplomatic יכול להיות גם רשמי (דיפלומטי) וגם יומיומי (טקטי). שני השימושים תקינים.

tip: 'Diplomatic' has two meanings: 1) relating to diplomacy (formal), 2) tactful (everyday). Both are very common.
tipHe: ל-diplomatic שתי משמעויות: 1) הקשור לדיפלומטיה (רשמי), 2) טקטי (יומיומי). שתיהן נפוצות מאוד.

definition: Protection granted to people fleeing persecution in their home country.
definitionHe: הגנה שניתנת לאנשים שבורחים מרדיפה במדינת מולדתם.
examples: She applied for political asylum.
The country granted asylum to the refugees.
Seeking asylum is a fundamental human right.

commonMistakes.explanation: אומרים apply for/seek asylum (לא ask an asylum).

tip: Use 'apply for asylum', 'seek asylum', or 'grant asylum'. Never 'ask an asylum'. 'Asylum' is uncountable in this context.
tipHe: אומרים apply for asylum, seek asylum, או grant asylum. אף פעם לא ask an asylum. asylum לא-ספירה בהקשר זה.

definition: The sending of a person accused of a crime back to the country where the crime was committed.
definitionHe: שליחת אדם החשוד בפשע חזרה למדינה שבה הפשע בוצע.
examples: The government approved the extradition request.
He is fighting extradition to the United States.
The extradition treaty covers serious crimes.

commonMistakes.wrong: He was extradited back to his country
commonMistakes.correct: He was extradited to his country
commonMistakes.explanation: back מיותר - extradited כבר מרמז על חזרה.

tip: 'Extradite' already implies returning someone - 'back' is redundant. The verb is 'extradite': 'They extradited him'.
tipHe: extradite כבר מרמז על החזרת מישהו - back מיותר. הפועל: extradite: They extradited him.

definition: To discuss terms and try to reach an agreement between parties.
definitionHe: לדון בתנאים ולנסות להגיע להסכם בין צדדים.
examples: The two leaders negotiated a trade deal.
They are negotiating the terms of the ceasefire.
She negotiated skillfully on behalf of her country.

commonMistakes.wrong: They negotiated for a long time about the deal
commonMistakes.correct: They negotiated the deal for a long time
commonMistakes.explanation: negotiate הוא יוצא ישיר: negotiate the deal (ללא about).

tip: 'Negotiate' takes a direct object: 'negotiate a deal/treaty' (no 'about'). The noun: 'negotiation'. Person: 'negotiator'.
tipHe: negotiate לוקח מושא ישיר: negotiate a deal/treaty (ללא about). שם עצם: negotiation. אדם: negotiator.

definition: A penalty or restriction imposed by one country on another.
definitionHe: עונש או הגבלה שמוטלים על ידי מדינה אחת על אחרת.
examples: The UN imposed sanctions on the regime.
Economic sanctions damaged the country's economy.
Sanctions were lifted after the agreement.

commonMistakes.wrong: They sanctioned the country with penalties
commonMistakes.correct: They imposed sanctions on the country
commonMistakes.explanation: sanction כפועל = לאשר או להטיל עונש (דו-משמעי). עדיף impose sanctions.

tip: Confusingly, 'sanction' as a verb can mean both 'approve' AND 'penalize'. Context determines meaning!
tipHe: מבלבל: sanction כפועל יכול לאמר גם 'לאשר' וגם 'להעניש'. ההקשר קובע את המשמעות!

definition: The authority of a state to govern itself without external interference.
definitionHe: סמכותה של מדינה לשלוט בעצמה ללא התערבות חיצונית.
examples: The country defended its sovereignty against foreign intervention.
Sovereignty is a cornerstone of international law.
The dispute centered on territorial sovereignty.

commonMistakes.wrong: The sovereignty of the country was threatened
commonMistakes.explanation: sovereignty נכתבת ללא g (לא soverignty). שימו לב לאיות המורכב.

tip: Tricky spelling: 'sovereignty' (not 'soverignty' or 'sovereignity'). The adjective is 'sovereign': a sovereign nation.
tipHe: איות מורכב: sovereignty (לא soverignty או sovereignity). התואר: sovereign: a sovereign nation.

definition: A period of significant decline in economic activity.
definitionHe: תקופה של ירידה משמעותית בפעילות כלכלית.
examples: The recession caused widespread unemployment.
The economy entered a recession in 2008.
Recovery from the recession took several years.

commonMistakes.explanation: affect (פועל) = להשפיע. effect (שם עצם) = השפעה. כאן צריך את הפועל: affected.

tip: 'Affect' = verb (to impact). 'Effect' = noun (the result). One of the most common English mistakes!
tipHe: affect = פועל (להשפיע). effect = שם עצם (התוצאה). אחת הטעויות הנפוצות ביותר באנגלית!

definition: Relating to government revenue, spending, and financial management.
definitionHe: הקשור להכנסות ממשלתיות, הוצאות וניהול פיננסי.
examples: The government announced its new fiscal policy.
The fiscal year ends in March.
Fiscal responsibility is crucial for economic stability.

commonMistakes.wrong: The fiscal politic of the government
commonMistakes.correct: The fiscal policy of the government
commonMistakes.explanation: policy (מדיניות) ולא politic (פוליטי). מילים שונות!

tip: 'Policy' = a plan/strategy. 'Politics' = the field/activity. 'Politic' (adj) = shrewd/wise. Don't confuse them!
tipHe: policy = תוכנית/אסטרטגיה. politics = התחום/הפעילות. politic (תואר) = חכם/ערום. אל תבלבלו!

definition: Relating to money or currency, especially as controlled by a central bank.
definitionHe: הקשור לכסף או למטבע, במיוחד כפי שנשלט על ידי בנק מרכזי.
examples: The central bank sets monetary policy.
Monetary stability is essential for growth.
They adopted strict monetary controls.

commonMistakes.explanation: policies ברבים עם ies (לא polices שזה שוטרים!).

tip: 'Policies' (plural of policy) vs 'polices' (third person of 'to police'). Completely different words!
tipHe: policies (רבים של policy) מול polices (גוף שלישי של to police). מילים שונות לגמרי!

definition: Money given by the government to help an industry or keep prices low.
definitionHe: כסף שניתן על ידי הממשלה לסייע לתעשייה או לשמור על מחירים נמוכים.
examples: Agricultural subsidies support local farmers.
The government cut subsidies for fossil fuels.
Subsidies help make education more affordable.

commonMistakes.wrong: The government gives subsidies on farmers
commonMistakes.correct: The government gives subsidies to farmers
commonMistakes.explanation: אומרים subsidies to (מיועדים ל-) ולא subsidies on.

tip: Use 'subsidies to/for' (recipients). The verb is 'subsidize': 'The government subsidizes education'.
tipHe: אומרים subsidies to/for (מקבלים). הפועל: subsidize: The government subsidizes education.

definition: A tax on goods imported from another country.
definitionHe: מס על סחורות שמיובאות ממדינה אחרת.
examples: The government imposed a tariff on steel imports.
High tariffs protect domestic industries.
They negotiated lower tariffs in the trade deal.

commonMistakes.wrong: They put a tarrif on imports
commonMistakes.correct: They imposed a tariff on imports
commonMistakes.explanation: שים לב: 1) tariff עם ff אחד. 2) אומרים impose a tariff (לא put).

tip: Spelling: 'tariff' (one 'r', two 'f's). Use 'impose/levy a tariff' (not 'put'). From Arabic 'ta'rif' (notification).
tipHe: איות: tariff (r אחד, שני f). אומרים impose/levy a tariff (לא put). מערבית ta'rif (הודעה).

definition: A general decrease in prices and increase in the value of money.
definitionHe: ירידה כללית במחירים ועלייה בערך הכסף.
examples: Deflation can be as harmful as inflation.
Japan experienced deflation for over a decade.
Deflation discourages spending and investment.

commonMistakes.explanation: אומרים deflation in (לא of) כשמדברים על המיקום של הדפלציה.

tip: 'Deflation' is the opposite of 'inflation'. 'Deflationary' is the adjective: 'deflationary spiral'.
tipHe: deflation הוא ההפך מ-inflation. deflationary הוא התואר: deflationary spiral.

definition: Strict economic measures to reduce government spending.
definitionHe: אמצעים כלכליים קפדניים להפחתת הוצאות הממשלה.
examples: The government implemented austerity measures.
Austerity policies led to public protests.
Years of austerity have affected public services.

commonMistakes.explanation: אומרים implement austerity measures/policies (לא make).

tip: Use 'implement/impose/introduce austerity measures' (not 'make'). 'Austere' is the adjective: an austere lifestyle.
tipHe: אומרים implement/impose/introduce austerity measures (לא make). austere הוא התואר: an austere lifestyle.

definition: Exclusive control of a commodity or service in a market.
definitionHe: שליטה בלעדית על סחורה או שירות בשוק.
examples: The company has a monopoly on the market.
Monopolies can lead to higher prices.
The government broke up the monopoly.

commonMistakes.wrong: They have a monopole on the market
commonMistakes.correct: They have a monopoly on the market
commonMistakes.explanation: שים לב לאיות: monopoly (לא monopole, שזה מושג פיזיקלי).

tip: 'Monopoly' uses 'on/over' (not 'of'). Adjective: 'monopolistic'. Verb: 'monopolize'. 'Monopole' is a physics term!
tipHe: monopoly משתמש ב-on/over (לא of). תואר: monopolistic. פועל: monopolize. monopole הוא מושג בפיזיקה!

definition: An amount that is more than what is needed or used.
definitionHe: כמות שהיא יותר ממה שנדרש או בשימוש.
examples: The country reported a trade surplus.
The budget surplus allowed for new investments.
They sold the surplus grain to other countries.

commonMistakes.wrong: There is a surplus of workers
commonMistakes.explanation: surplus of = עודף של. surplus הוא גם שם עצם וגם תואר: surplus stock.

tip: 'Surplus' is both noun and adjective: 'a surplus' (noun) or 'surplus stock' (adjective). Opposite: 'deficit'.
tipHe: surplus הוא גם שם עצם וגם תואר: a surplus (שם עצם) או surplus stock (תואר). הפוך: deficit.

definition: A shortage or lack of something.
definitionHe: מחסור או חוסר של משהו.
examples: Water scarcity is a growing global problem.
The scarcity of resources led to higher prices.
Scarcity drives innovation.

commonMistakes.wrong: There is a scarity of food
commonMistakes.correct: There is a scarcity of food
commonMistakes.explanation: שים לב לאיות: scarcity עם c (לא scarity).

tip: Spelling: 'scarcity' (from 'scarce'). 'Scarcity' is a fundamental concept in economics - it drives supply and demand.
tipHe: איות: scarcity (מ-scarce). scarcity הוא מושג יסודי בכלכלה - הוא מניע היצע וביקוש.

definition: The study of individual economic units such as households and firms.
definitionHe: חקר יחידות כלכליות בודדות כגון משקי בית וחברות.
examples: Microeconomics focuses on supply and demand.
She took a course in microeconomics.
Microeconomics examines individual market behavior.

commonMistakes.explanation: microeconomics מסתיים ב-s אבל הוא יחיד → is.

tip: Words ending in '-ics' (economics, physics, mathematics) take singular verbs: 'Microeconomics is...' (not 'are').
tipHe: מילים שמסתיימות ב-ics (economics, physics, mathematics) לוקחות פועל יחיד: Microeconomics is... (לא are).

definition: The study of the economy as a whole, including GDP, inflation, and unemployment.
definitionHe: חקר הכלכלה כמכלול, כולל תמ"ג, אינפלציה ואבטלה.
examples: Macroeconomics deals with national economic policies.
He specializes in macroeconomics.
Macroeconomics examines aggregate economic indicators.

commonMistakes.explanation: indicators הוא רבים → show (ללא s).

tip: Subject-verb agreement: plural 'indicators' takes 'show' (not 'shows'). 'Macro-' = large-scale, 'micro-' = small-scale.
tipHe: התאמת נושא-פועל: indicators ברבים לוקח show (ללא s). macro- = גדול, micro- = קטן.

definition: Evaluation of work by experts in the same field before publication.
definitionHe: הערכת עבודה על ידי מומחים באותו תחום לפני פרסום.
examples: The article underwent peer review before publication.
Peer review ensures the quality of research.
The peer-review process can take several months.

commonMistakes.explanation: peer-reviewed (עם ed) הוא התואר. peer review (ללא ed) הוא שם עצם.

tip: 'Peer-reviewed' = adjective (a peer-reviewed journal). 'Peer review' = noun (the process). Don't mix them up.
tipHe: peer-reviewed = תואר (peer-reviewed journal). peer review = שם עצם (התהליך). אל תבלבלו.

definition: Relating to the quality or character of something, not its quantity.
definitionHe: הקשור לאיכות או לאופי של משהו, לא לכמותו.
examples: They conducted qualitative research through interviews.
Qualitative data provides deeper understanding.
The study used a qualitative approach.

commonMistakes.explanation: שים לב לאיות: qualitative (לא qualitive).

tip: Spelling: 'qualitative' (not 'qualitive'). Opposite: 'quantitative'. Both approaches are valid in research.
tipHe: איות: qualitative (לא qualitive). הפוך: quantitative. שתי הגישות תקפות במחקר.

definition: Relating to measuring amounts or quantities using numbers.
definitionHe: הקשור למדידת כמויות או סכומים באמצעות מספרים.
examples: Quantitative analysis uses statistical methods.
The study collected quantitative data from surveys.
Quantitative research measures numerical outcomes.

commonMistakes.explanation: שים לב לאיות: quantitative (לא quantative).

tip: Spelling: 'quantitative' (not 'quantative'). Remember: 'quanti-' from 'quantity' + '-tative'.
tipHe: איות: quantitative (לא quantative). זכרו: quanti- מ-quantity + -tative.

definition: Studying subjects over a long period of time.
definitionHe: חוקר נבדקים לאורך תקופה ארוכה.
examples: The longitudinal study tracked participants for 20 years.
Longitudinal data reveals trends over time.
A longitudinal approach provides richer insights.

commonMistakes.explanation: 1) longitudinal (לא longitudal). 2) אומרים conducted a study (לא did).

tip: Spelling: 'longitudinal' (not 'longitudal'). Opposite: 'cross-sectional' (studying at one point in time).
tipHe: איות: longitudinal (לא longitudal). הפוך: cross-sectional (חוקר בנקודת זמן אחת).

definition: To repeat an experiment or study to verify results.
definitionHe: לחזור על ניסוי או מחקר כדי לאמת תוצאות.
examples: Other researchers tried to replicate the findings.
The experiment was replicated in three laboratories.
Results that cannot be replicated are unreliable.

commonMistakes.wrong: They tried to replicate on the results
commonMistakes.correct: They tried to replicate the results
commonMistakes.explanation: replicate הוא יוצא - replicate something (ללא on).

tip: 'Replicate' is transitive: 'replicate the results' (no 'on'). The 'replication crisis' is a major issue in science.
tipHe: replicate הוא יוצא: replicate the results (ללא on). 'משבר השחזור' הוא בעיה מרכזית במדע.

definition: A sample taken for examination or study.
definitionHe: דגימה שנלקחת לבדיקה או מחקר.
examples: The scientist examined the specimen under a microscope.
Blood specimens were collected from all participants.
The museum has rare specimens of butterflies.

commonMistakes.explanation: שים לב לאיות: specimens (לא specimans).

tip: Spelling: 'specimen' (not 'speciman'). Plural: 'specimens'. Similar to 'sample' but more formal/scientific.
tipHe: איות: specimen (לא speciman). רבים: specimens. דומה ל-sample אבל רשמי/מדעי יותר.

definition: A departure from an established norm or standard.
definitionHe: סטייה מנורמה או סטנדרט מבוססים.
examples: The results showed a significant deviation from the mean.
Standard deviation measures data spread.
Any deviation from the protocol must be reported.

commonMistakes.wrong: The deviation of the results is big
commonMistakes.correct: The deviation in the results is significant
commonMistakes.explanation: 1) deviation in (לא of) לתוצאות. 2) אומרים significant (לא big) במחקר.

tip: Use 'significant deviation' (not 'big') in research. 'Standard deviation' is a key statistical term.
tipHe: אומרים significant deviation (לא big) במחקר. standard deviation הוא מונח סטטיסטי חשוב.

definition: A standard or point of reference for measuring performance.
definitionHe: סטנדרט או נקודת ייחוס למדידת ביצועים.
examples: The company set new benchmarks for quality.
This score serves as a benchmark for future tests.
Industry benchmarks help compare performance.

commonMistakes.wrong: They benchmarked with last year's results
commonMistakes.correct: They benchmarked against last year's results
commonMistakes.explanation: אומרים benchmark against (לא with).

tip: Use 'benchmark against' (compare to a standard). 'Benchmark' is both noun and verb: 'to benchmark performance'.
tipHe: אומרים benchmark against (השוואה לסטנדרט). benchmark הוא גם שם עצם וגם פועל: to benchmark performance.

definition: A measurable factor that defines a system or sets conditions.
definitionHe: גורם מדיד שמגדיר מערכת או קובע תנאים.
examples: The parameters of the study were clearly defined.
You can adjust the parameters to change the output.
Temperature is a key parameter in this experiment.

commonMistakes.explanation: parameter ביחיד, parameters ברבים - יש להתאים.

tip: Don't confuse 'parameter' (a measurable limit) with 'perimeter' (the boundary of an area). Different words!
tipHe: אל תבלבלו parameter (גבול מדיד) עם perimeter (היקף של שטח). מילים שונות!

definition: The quality of being logically sound or based on good reasoning.
definitionHe: האיכות של להיות הגיוני או מבוסס על חשיבה טובה.
examples: The validity of the research was questioned.
Internal validity ensures the study measures what it claims.
They tested the validity of the instrument.

commonMistakes.explanation: validity הוא שם העצם הנכון (לא validness).

tip: 'Validity' (not 'validness') is the standard noun. 'Valid' = adjective. 'Validate' = verb. 'Validation' = the act.
tipHe: validity (לא validness) הוא שם העצם הסטנדרטי. valid = תואר. validate = פועל. validation = הפעולה.

definition: A brief summary of a research paper or article.
definitionHe: תקציר קצר של מאמר מחקרי או כתבה.
examples: The abstract summarizes the key findings.
Read the abstract before the full paper.
Submit a 300-word abstract for the conference.

commonMistakes.wrong: He wrote an abstract of 300 words about his research
commonMistakes.correct: He wrote a 300-word abstract of his research
commonMistakes.explanation: עדיף 300-word abstract (תואר מורכב) ולא abstract of 300 words.

tip: As a noun (AB-strakt) = summary. As an adjective (ab-STRAKT) = not concrete. As a verb (ab-STRAKT) = to remove.
tipHe: כשם עצם (AB-strakt) = תקציר. כתואר (ab-STRAKT) = מופשט. כפועל (ab-STRAKT) = לחלץ.

definition: A reference to a source of information in academic writing.
definitionHe: הפניה למקור מידע בכתיבה אקדמית.
examples: The paper includes 50 citations.
Proper citation prevents plagiarism.
The citation format follows APA style.

commonMistakes.wrong: He forgot to put a citation
commonMistakes.correct: He forgot to include a citation
commonMistakes.explanation: אומרים include a citation (לא put) בהקשר אקדמי.

tip: Use 'include/add a citation'. 'Cite' is the verb: 'cite a source'. Don't confuse 'citation' with 'quotation'.
tipHe: אומרים include/add a citation. cite הוא הפועל: cite a source. אל תבלבלו citation עם quotation.

definition: A collection of related data organized for analysis.
definitionHe: אוסף של נתונים קשורים המאורגנים לניתוח.
examples: The dataset contains information from 10,000 participants.
They cleaned the dataset before analysis.
The dataset is publicly available online.

commonMistakes.explanation: data הוא כבר רבים (יחיד: datum). לא אומרים datas.

tip: 'Data' is technically plural (singular: 'datum'), but modern usage accepts it as uncountable: 'The data is/are...'
tipHe: data הוא טכנית רבים (יחיד: datum), אבל שימוש מודרני מקבל אותו כלא-ספיר: The data is/are...

definition: The quality of being trusted and believed in.
definitionHe: התכונה של להיות מהימן ולהיות מאמינים בו.
examples: The scandal damaged his credibility.
Credibility is essential for journalists.
The source lacks credibility.

commonMistakes.explanation: שים לב לאיות: credibility (לא credibilty).

tip: 'Credibility' is uncountable. Adjective: 'credible' (believable). Don't confuse with 'creditable' (deserving praise).
tipHe: credibility היא לא-ספירה. תואר: credible (אמין). אל תבלבלו עם creditable (ראוי לשבח).

definition: The quality of being fair and not influenced by personal feelings.
definitionHe: התכונה של להיות הוגן ולא מושפע מרגשות אישיים.
examples: Journalists should strive for objectivity.
Complete objectivity is difficult to achieve.
The report was praised for its objectivity.

commonMistakes.explanation: objectivity (לא objectiveness) הוא שם העצם הסטנדרטי.

tip: 'Objectivity' (not 'objectiveness') is the standard noun. Adjective: 'objective'. Opposite: 'subjectivity'.
tipHe: objectivity (לא objectiveness) הוא שם העצם הסטנדרטי. תואר: objective. הפוך: subjectivity.

definition: The use of exciting or shocking stories to attract attention.
definitionHe: שימוש בסיפורים מרגשים או מזעזעים כדי למשוך תשומת לב.
examples: The tabloid is known for its sensationalism.
Sensationalism undermines serious journalism.
She accused the media of sensationalism.

commonMistakes.wrong: The news is full of sensationality
commonMistakes.correct: The news is full of sensationalism
commonMistakes.explanation: sensationalism (לא sensationality) הוא שם העצם הנכון.

tip: 'Sensationalism' (not 'sensationality'). Adjective: 'sensational' or 'sensationalist'. Related to 'tabloid journalism'.
tipHe: sensationalism (לא sensationality). תואר: sensational או sensationalist. קשור ל-tabloid journalism.

definition: A person who reveals illegal or unethical activity in an organization.
definitionHe: אדם שחושף פעילות בלתי חוקית או לא אתית בארגון.
examples: The whistleblower exposed government corruption.
Whistleblower protection laws exist in many countries.
She became a whistleblower after discovering fraud.

commonMistakes.explanation: whistleblower נכתבת כמילה אחת.

tip: 'Whistleblower' is one word (sometimes hyphenated: whistle-blower). The act is 'whistleblowing'.
tipHe: whistleblower היא מילה אחת (לפעמים עם מקף: whistle-blower). הפעולה: whistleblowing.

definition: The suppression of speech, writing, or information by authorities.
definitionHe: דיכוי של דיבור, כתיבה או מידע על ידי רשויות.
examples: Government censorship limits press freedom.
Internet censorship is widespread in some countries.
The author spoke against censorship.

commonMistakes.wrong: The censorship of the media are strict
commonMistakes.correct: The censorship of the media is strict
commonMistakes.explanation: censorship הוא יחיד → is.

tip: 'Censorship' is uncountable and singular: 'censorship is...' 'Censor' = the person or verb. 'Censorship' = the system/practice.
tipHe: censorship היא לא-ספירה ויחיד: censorship is... censor = האדם או הפועל. censorship = המערכת/הנוהג.

definition: Relating to detailed examination or inquiry, especially in journalism.
definitionHe: הקשור לבדיקה או חקירה מפורטת, במיוחד בעיתונות.
examples: Investigative journalism exposed the corruption.
She is an investigative reporter.
The investigative team spent months on the story.

commonMistakes.wrong: The journalist made an investigative story
commonMistakes.correct: The journalist wrote an investigative story
commonMistakes.explanation: אומרים write/publish a story (לא make) בהקשר עיתונאי.

tip: In journalism, 'write/break/publish a story' (not 'make'). 'Break a story' = be the first to report it.
tipHe: בעיתונאות, אומרים write/break/publish a story (לא make). break a story = לדווח ראשון.

definition: The quality of being open, honest, and easy to understand.
definitionHe: התכונה של להיות פתוח, ישר וקל להבנה.
examples: The government promised greater transparency.
Transparency in reporting builds public trust.
Financial transparency is required by law.

commonMistakes.explanation: transparency (לא transparence) הוא שם העצם הסטנדרטי באנגלית.

tip: 'Transparency' (not 'transparence') is standard English. Adjective: 'transparent'. Used both literally (glass) and figuratively (openness).
tipHe: transparency (לא transparence) היא אנגלית סטנדרטית. תואר: transparent. משמש גם מילולית (זכוכית) וגם מטאפורית (פתיחות).

definition: The action of damaging someone's reputation by making false statements.
definitionHe: הפעולה של פגיעה בשם הטוב של מישהו על ידי אמירת דברים שקריים.
examples: He filed a defamation lawsuit against the newspaper.
Defamation can be either libel or slander.
The journalist was accused of defamation.

commonMistakes.wrong: He sued the journalist for defamating him
commonMistakes.correct: He sued the journalist for defaming him
commonMistakes.explanation: הפועל הוא defame (לא defamate). defaming הוא ה-gerund.

tip: The verb is 'defame' (not 'defamate'). 'Libel' = written defamation. 'Slander' = spoken defamation.
tipHe: הפועל הוא defame (לא defamate). libel = הוצאת דיבה בכתב. slander = הוצאת דיבה בעל פה.

definition: A public withdrawal of a statement or article because it was wrong.
definitionHe: נסיגה פומבית מהצהרה או כתבה כי היא הייתה שגויה.
examples: The newspaper published a retraction of the story.
The scientist's retraction shocked the academic world.
They demanded a retraction from the editor.

commonMistakes.wrong: The newspaper retracted back the article
commonMistakes.explanation: retract כבר אומר לקחת חזרה, back מיותר.

tip: 'Retract' already means to take back - 'back' is redundant. The verb is 'retract': 'retract a statement'.
tipHe: retract כבר אומר לקחת חזרה - back מיותר. הפועל: retract: retract a statement.

definition: Not identified by name; of unknown identity.
definitionHe: לא מזוהה בשם; בזהות לא ידועה.
examples: The source wished to remain anonymous.
An anonymous donation funded the project.
The anonymous tip led to an arrest.

commonMistakes.explanation: 1) anonymous (לא anonymus). 2) אומרים remain anonymous (לא stay).

tip: Spelling: 'anonymous' (not 'anonymus'). Use 'remain anonymous' (more formal than 'stay'). Noun: 'anonymity'.
tipHe: איות: anonymous (לא anonymus). אומרים remain anonymous (רשמי יותר מ-stay). שם עצם: anonymity.

definition: A situation in which a difficult choice has to be made between two options.
definitionHe: מצב שבו יש לעשות בחירה קשה בין שתי אפשרויות.
examples: The doctor faced an ethical dilemma.
It's a real dilemma - both options have drawbacks.
The dilemma of privacy vs. security is ongoing.

commonMistakes.explanation: 1) dilemma ולא dilemna (ללא n). 2) serious/difficult dilemma (לא big). 3) face a dilemma.

tip: Spelling: 'dilemma' (not 'dilemna' - common error). Use 'face/confront a dilemma' (not 'have').
tipHe: איות: dilemma (לא dilemna - טעות נפוצה). אומרים face/confront a dilemma (לא have).

definition: The obligation to accept responsibility for one's actions.
definitionHe: החובה לקבל אחריות על מעשיו.
examples: There must be accountability for these failures.
The new system increases accountability.
Public officials must be held to accountability.

commonMistakes.explanation: שים לב לאיות: accountability (לא accountablity).

tip: 'Accountability' is uncountable. 'Hold someone accountable' is the key phrase. Adjective: 'accountable'.
tipHe: accountability היא לא-ספירה. hold someone accountable הוא ביטוי מפתח. תואר: accountable.

definition: Permission or agreement given voluntarily.
definitionHe: רשות או הסכמה שניתנות מרצון.
examples: Patients must give informed consent before surgery.
She gave her consent to the arrangement.
Age of consent varies by country.

commonMistakes.wrong: He consented them to use his data
commonMistakes.correct: He consented to them using his data
commonMistakes.explanation: consent הוא עומד - consent to something (לא consent someone).

tip: 'Consent' is intransitive: 'consent to something' (not 'consent something'). Both noun and verb.
tipHe: consent הוא עומד: consent to something (לא consent something). גם שם עצם וגם פועל.

definition: Selfless concern for the well-being of others.
definitionHe: דאגה חסרת אינטרס לרווחת הזולת.
examples: Her altruism inspired everyone around her.
Altruism is valued in many cultures.
Pure altruism means expecting nothing in return.

commonMistakes.explanation: altruism היא לא-ספירה - ללא a.

tip: 'Altruism' is uncountable (no 'a'). Adjective: 'altruistic'. Opposite: 'egoism' or 'selfishness'.
tipHe: altruism היא לא-ספירה (ללא a). תואר: altruistic. הפוך: egoism או selfishness.

definition: Treating all people and sides equally; not biased.
definitionHe: מתייחס לכל האנשים והצדדים באופן שווה; ללא הטיה.
examples: The judge must remain impartial.
An impartial investigation was launched.
It's hard to be impartial when friends are involved.

commonMistakes.wrong: The judge was not impartial to the defendant
commonMistakes.correct: The judge was not impartial toward the defendant
commonMistakes.explanation: אומרים impartial toward (לא to).

tip: 'Impartial' ≠ 'unpartial' (which doesn't exist). Prefix 'im-' means 'not'. Noun: 'impartiality'.
tipHe: impartial ≠ unpartial (שלא קיימת). הקידומת im- = לא. שם עצם: impartiality.

definition: The right of self-governance; personal independence.
definitionHe: הזכות לשלטון עצמי; עצמאות אישית.
examples: Patient autonomy is a core medical principle.
The region was granted greater autonomy.
Personal autonomy means making your own choices.

commonMistakes.wrong: The autonomy of the patient are important
commonMistakes.correct: The autonomy of the patient is important
commonMistakes.explanation: autonomy הוא יחיד → is.

tip: 'Autonomy' is singular/uncountable → 'is'. Adjective: 'autonomous'. From Greek: auto (self) + nomos (law).
tipHe: autonomy הוא יחיד/לא-ספיר → is. תואר: autonomous. מיוונית: auto (עצמי) + nomos (חוק).

definition: The state of keeping information private and secret.
definitionHe: המצב של שמירת מידע פרטי וסודי.
examples: Doctors must maintain patient confidentiality.
The agreement includes a confidentiality clause.
Breaching confidentiality can have legal consequences.

commonMistakes.explanation: שים לב לאיות: confidentiality (לא confidenciality).

tip: Spelling: 'confidentiality' (from 'confidential'). 'Breach of confidentiality' = violation of privacy. NDA = non-disclosure agreement.
tipHe: איות: confidentiality (מ-confidential). breach of confidentiality = הפרת סודיות. NDA = הסכם סודיות.

definition: The act of using someone or something unfairly for one's own benefit.
definitionHe: הפעולה של שימוש במישהו או במשהו באופן לא הוגן למען תועלת עצמית.
examples: Labor exploitation remains a global problem.
The exploitation of natural resources must be controlled.
She spoke against the exploitation of workers.

commonMistakes.explanation: אומרים exploitation of (לא on).

tip: 'Exploitation of' (not 'on'). The verb 'exploit' can be positive (exploit an opportunity) or negative (exploit workers).
tipHe: exploitation of (לא on). הפועל exploit יכול להיות חיובי (exploit an opportunity) או שלילי (exploit workers).

definition: The act of following rules, laws, or regulations.
definitionHe: הפעולה של מעקב אחר כללים, חוקים או תקנות.
examples: The company is in compliance with the regulations.
Compliance with safety standards is mandatory.
The compliance department monitors adherence to rules.

commonMistakes.wrong: The company is in a compliance with the law
commonMistakes.correct: The company is in compliance with the law
commonMistakes.explanation: in compliance with ללא a - זה ביטוי קבוע.

tip: 'In compliance with' (no 'a') is a fixed phrase. Adjective: 'compliant'. Don't confuse with 'compliment'!
tipHe: in compliance with (ללא a) הוא ביטוי קבוע. תואר: compliant. אל תבלבלו עם compliment!

definition: The practice of intentionally ending a life to relieve suffering.
definitionHe: הנוהג של סיום חיים במכוון כדי להקל על סבל.
examples: Euthanasia remains illegal in most countries.
The debate over euthanasia is highly emotional.
Active euthanasia involves administering a lethal substance.

commonMistakes.explanation: שים לב לאיות: euthanasia עם th (לא t לבד).

tip: Spelling: 'euthanasia' (with 'th'). From Greek: eu (good) + thanatos (death). Also called 'mercy killing'.
tipHe: איות: euthanasia (עם th). מיוונית: eu (טוב) + thanatos (מוות). נקרא גם mercy killing.

definition: The quality of being honest and having strong moral principles.
definitionHe: התכונה של להיות ישר ובעל עקרונות מוסריים חזקים.
examples: Academic integrity means doing your own work.
His integrity is beyond question.
Integrity is one of the most valued traits in a leader.

commonMistakes.explanation: integrity לא-ספירה. אומרים great/strong integrity (לא big).

tip: 'Integrity' also means 'structural wholeness': 'the integrity of the building'. Two meanings, one word.
tipHe: integrity גם אומר 'שלמות מבנית': the integrity of the building. שתי משמעויות, מילה אחת.

definition: The act of reporting illegal or unethical activities within an organization.
definitionHe: הפעולה של דיווח על פעילויות בלתי חוקיות או לא אתיות בתוך ארגון.
examples: Whistleblowing is protected by law in many countries.
The act of whistleblowing can be risky.
She was praised for her whistleblowing.

commonMistakes.explanation: whistleblowing נכתבת כמילה אחת.

tip: 'Whistleblowing' = the act. 'Whistleblower' = the person. Both are one word. Increasingly important in corporate ethics.
tipHe: whistleblowing = הפעולה. whistleblower = האדם. שניהם מילה אחת. חשוב יותר ויותר באתיקה ארגונית.

definition: The ethical theory that the best action is the one that maximizes well-being for all.
definitionHe: התיאוריה האתית שלפיה הפעולה הטובה ביותר היא זו שממקסמת רווחה לכולם.
examples: Utilitarianism was developed by Jeremy Bentham.
According to utilitarianism, the ends justify the means.
Utilitarianism focuses on consequences rather than intentions.

commonMistakes.explanation: שים לב לאיות המלא: utilitarianism (לא utilitarism).

tip: Spelling: 'utilitarianism' (not 'utilitarism'). Adjective: 'utilitarian'. Contrasts with 'deontological ethics' (duty-based).
tipHe: איות: utilitarianism (לא utilitarism). תואר: utilitarian. בניגוד ל-deontological ethics (אתיקה חובתית).

definition: The study of ethical issues arising from advances in biology and medicine.
definitionHe: חקר הסוגיות האתיות הנובעות מהתקדמות בביולוגיה וברפואה.
examples: Bioethics addresses issues like genetic engineering.
The bioethics committee reviewed the proposal.
She teaches a course on bioethics.

commonMistakes.wrong: The bio-ethics is a new field
commonMistakes.correct: Bioethics is a relatively new field
commonMistakes.explanation: bioethics נכתבת כמילה אחת ללא מקף.

tip: 'Bioethics' is one word (no hyphen). Like other '-ics' words, it takes a singular verb: 'Bioethics is...'
tipHe: bioethics היא מילה אחת (ללא מקף). כמו מילות ics אחרות, לוקחת פועל יחיד: Bioethics is...
